title: KEEL Binder
description: Framework-frame schema for a KEEL Binder. Validates Binder-level structure, the feature frame, oracle shape, and cross-reference integrity. The per-feature `contract` object is intentionally open-shape — contract content is case-by-case and not generalizable. Feature-specific contract gaps are detected by downstream agents (test-writer) via expected-vs-declared key introspection, not by schema enforcement.

schema_version.description: Binder schema version. Bumps only on breaking shape changes; validators pin to a supported set. If a Binder declares a version this validator doesn't know, the halt message names the mismatch and the schema path the Binder needs to be valid against.

id.description: Slug form; mirrors the Binder filename stem and the slug alphabet in scripts/keel-binder-view.py.

motivation.description: Single narrative field. One short paragraph on why this feature set exists. Not a dumping ground — if it exceeds 800 chars, the content belongs in design_facts or scope.included.

needs.description: Other feature IDs this one depends on. Cross-reference integrity (each ref must resolve to an existing feature) is enforced by the post-schema validator, not by the schema alone.

contract.description: Feature-specific contract content. Intentionally open-shape: a trigger feature needs channel+payload_fields; a REST endpoint needs route+auth+response; a UI module needs module_path+public_api. No cross-feature generalization survives, so no required keys are declared here. Feature-specific gaps (e.g. a trigger feature missing 'channel') are detected by test-writer via expected-vs-declared key introspection at pipeline time.

type.description: Test abstraction level. Framework/runner identity (vitest, pytest, etc.) belongs in `tooling`, not here.

tooling.description: Free-form: framework, mocks, fixtures, timers, etc.

gating.description: Free-form note e.g. 'CI merge-blocking' when the test is a required gate, or scope clarifications.
